metadata.title: Citation Process Pipeline Documentation
metadata.description: Comprehensive documentation of the AI-powered citation system for generating, managing, and formatting academic citations
metadata.prompt: Explain the full citation process that happens in terms of the AI usage and overall pipeline in the code

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Citera citation system implements a comprehensive AI-powered pipeline for generating, managing, and formatting academic citations. This documentation details the complete citation workflow across two primary interfaces: **Basic AI Citation** (`/ai-citation`) and **Premium AI Citation Assistant** (`/ai-citation-pro`).

sections.title: Architecture Components
sections.content: The system consists of multiple interconnected components that work together to provide citation functionality.

subsections.title: Frontend Citation Interfaces
subsections.content: The frontend provides two distinct interfaces for different use cases.

subsections.title: Basic Citation Interface
subsections.content: The `Citation()` function component implements a read-only citation viewer with PDF highlighting capabilities. This interface is designed for users who need to view and format citations without editing capabilities.
subsections.technical_details: The component uses React hooks for state management, including `useState` for managing references, paper details, and UI state. It extracts the `fileId` from URL query parameters using `useSearchParams()` and waits for PDF load before querying Supabase for citation data.
notes: The component implements deduplication logic to ensure unique references are displayed
PDF highlighting is enabled by default but can be toggled by users

subsections.title: Premium Citation Interface
subsections.content: The `CitationContent()` function implements an advanced interactive editor with citation manipulation capabilities. This interface provides full control over citations including reciting, rephrasing, and removing citations.
subsections.technical_details: The premium interface manages both `references` and `cited_sentences` arrays from Supabase `files_pro` table. It includes state management for highlighted sentences, expanded sentences, and loading states for AI operations.
notes: Requires Supabase `files_pro` table access
Integrates with Tiptap editor for real-time content updates

subsections.title: Citation Data Pipeline
subsections.content: The citation data pipeline handles fetching and processing paper metadata from external APIs.

subsections.title: Reference Fetching Workflow
subsections.content: Both interfaces implement a two-stage fetching process with error recovery to ensure reliable data retrieval.

subsections.title: Stage 1: Batch Fetching
subsections.content: The system first attempts to fetch multiple papers in a single API call using Semantic Scholar's batch endpoint. This is more efficient but can fail due to rate limiting.
subsections.technical_details: The batch API endpoint is `https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/batch?fields=title,authors,year,url,abstract,fieldsOfStudy,publicationDate`. If the response status is 429 (rate limited) or >= 500 (server error), the system automatically falls back to individual fetching.
notes: Requires `SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY` environment variable
Batch requests are limited to a maximum number of papers per request

subsections.title: Stage 2: Individual Fallback
subsections.content: If batch fetching fails, the system falls back to fetching papers individually with rate limiting protection.
subsections.technical_details: The individual fallback implements a 500ms delay between requests to prevent rate limiting. It performs progressive state updates for better UX and gracefully degrades to basic metadata if detailed information is unavailable.
notes: Individual fetching is slower but more reliable
Progressive updates provide better user experience during long operations

subsections.title: Citation Formatting System
subsections.content: The system supports multiple academic citation styles with conditional formatting logic.

subsections.title: Multi-Style Formatter
subsections.content: The `formatCitation` function implements four academic citation styles: APA, MLA, Chicago, and Harvard. Each style has specific formatting rules for author lists, dates, and citation structure.
subsections.technical_details: Author list formatting varies by style. APA and Harvard use ampersands for multiple authors, while MLA and Chicago use 'and'. The function handles edge cases like single authors, two authors, and multiple authors (using 'et al.').
notes: Citation styles follow official academic formatting guidelines
Date formatting includes fallback to current date if publication date is unavailable

subsections.title: Premium Features: AI Citation Manipulation
subsections.content: The premium interface provides advanced AI-powered features for manipulating citations.

subsections.title: Recite Functionality
subsections.content: The recite feature allows users to replace a citation with an alternative source while maintaining sentence context. This is useful when a better source is found for the same claim.
subsections.technical_details: The `handleRecite` function sends the cited sentence and file ID to `/api/recite` endpoint. The API returns a new citation and paper ID. The function then updates the highlighted sentences array and syncs with the Tiptap editor before saving to Supabase.
notes: The original sentence structure is preserved
Only the citation portion is replaced

subsections.title: Rephrase Workflow
subsections.content: The rephrase feature generates alternative phrasings for cited sentences, allowing users to improve clarity or avoid repetition.
subsections.technical_details: The rephrase workflow is a two-phase process: (1) Generate options via `/api/rephrase` endpoint, (2) Apply selected option via `/api/apply-rephrase` endpoint. The system updates both the UI state and the Tiptap editor content atomically.
notes: Multiple rephrase options are generated for user selection
Changes are immediately reflected in the editor

classes.name: CitationContent
classes.description: Advanced interactive citation editor component with AI-powered manipulation capabilities.

attributes.name: highlightedSentences
attributes.description: Array of sentences containing citations, with highlighting state and metadata.

attributes.name: expandedSentence
attributes.description: Currently expanded sentence ID for showing detailed citation options.

attributes.name: loadingRecite
attributes.description: Loading state for recite operations, keyed by sentence ID.

attributes.name: loadingRephrase
attributes.description: Loading state for rephrase operations, keyed by sentence ID.

methods.name: handleRecite
methods.description: Replace citation with alternative source while maintaining sentence context.

parameters.name: citedSentence
parameters.description: The sentence containing the citation to replace. Must include the citation in parentheses.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the document file in Supabase.

returns.description: Updates UI state and syncs with Tiptap editor. Does not return a value.

examples.description: Replace citation in a sentence with a new source

notes: The function extracts the sentence without citation before appending the new citation
Updates are persisted to Supabase after editor sync

methods.name: handleRephrase
methods.description: Generate alternative phrasings for a cited sentence.

parameters.name: citedSentence
parameters.description: The sentence to rephrase, including its citation.

returns.description: Opens modal with rephrase options. Does not return a value.

examples.code: await handleRephrase('Machine learning is transforming research (Smith, 2020).');
examples.description: Generate rephrase options for a sentence

notes: This is the first phase of the rephrase workflow
User must call `applyRephrase` to apply a selected option

methods.name: applyRephrase
methods.description: Apply a selected rephrase option to replace the original sentence.

parameters.name: selectedOption
parameters.description: The rephrased sentence option selected by the user.

returns.description: Updates sentence in UI and editor, then saves to Supabase.

examples.code: await applyRephrase('Research is being revolutionized by machine learning (Smith, 2020).');

notes: This is the second phase of the rephrase workflow
Must be called after `handleRephrase` generates options

methods.name: handleRemoveSentence
methods.description: Remove a cited sentence from the document with optimistic UI updates.

parameters.name: sentence
parameters.description: The sentence to remove from the document.

returns.description: Removes sentence from UI and editor, with error recovery on failure.

examples.code: await handleRemoveSentence('Machine learning is transforming research (Smith, 2020).');
examples.description: Remove a cited sentence from the document

notes: Implements optimistic UI updates for better UX
Automatically recovers from errors by reloading from Supabase

examples.description: Basic usage of the CitationContent component

classes.name: Citation
classes.description: Read-only citation viewer component with PDF highlighting capabilities.

attributes.name: uniqueReferences
attributes.description: Deduplicated list of paper references extracted from the document.

attributes.name: paperDetails
attributes.description: Cached paper metadata from Semantic Scholar API, keyed by paper ID.

attributes.name: highlightEnabled
attributes.description: Whether PDF highlighting is currently enabled.

examples.description: Basic usage - component automatically extracts fileId from URL

see_also.name: CitationContent
see_also.description: Premium citation editor with manipulation features

functions.name: formatCitation
functions.description: Format citation in multiple academic styles (APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard).

parameters.name: paperId
parameters.description: Semantic Scholar paper ID or internal paper identifier.

parameters.name: style
parameters.description: Citation style to use. Must be one of: 'APA' | 'MLA' | 'Chicago' | 'Harvard'.

returns.description: Formatted citation string according to the specified style.

examples.code:
const citation = formatCitation('paper-123', 'APA');
// Returns: "Smith, J. (2020). Machine Learning in Research. Journal of AI, 15(3)."

const citation = formatCitation('paper-123', 'MLA');
// Returns: "Smith, John. \"Machine Learning in Research.\" Journal of AI, 15.3 (2020)."

notes: Supports four academic citation styles with conditional formatting logic
Author list formatting varies by style (ampersand vs 'and')
Handles edge cases: single author, two authors, multiple authors (et al.)

see_also.name: fetchPaperDetailsBatch
see_also.description: Fetch paper metadata for citation formatting

functions.name: fetchPaperDetailsBatch
functions.description: Fetch multiple paper details from Semantic Scholar API in a single batch request.

parameters.name: paperIds
parameters.description: Array of Semantic Scholar paper IDs to fetch.

returns.description: Promise resolving to a record of paper details, keyed by paper ID.

examples.description: Fetch multiple papers in a single request

notes: Automatically falls back to individual fetching if batch request fails
Requires SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY environment variable
Rate limited - may return 429 status if too many requests

see_also.name: fetchPaperDetailsIndividual
see_also.description: Fallback method for individual paper fetching

functions.name: fetchPaperDetailsIndividual
functions.description: Fetch paper details individually with rate limiting protection.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Array of reference objects containing paper IDs.

returns.description: Promise resolving to a record of paper details, keyed by paper ID.

examples.description: Fetch papers individually with 500ms delay between requests

notes: Implements 500ms delay between requests to prevent rate limiting
Performs progressive state updates for better UX
Gracefully degrades to basic metadata if detailed info unavailable
